functions.name: talisman_tools.configure.wrapper.actions.wrap_model_action
documentation:
create model from config and wraps it with respect to specified wrapper and wrapper configuration
\:param config: dict with required "wrapper" and "to_wrap" keys. Optional "plugin" and "config" keys are taken into account
\:return: wrapped document processor

functions.name: talisman_tools.configure.wrapper.actions.configure_model_action
documentation:
create model from config
\:param config: dict with required "model" key
\:return: configured model

classes.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.evaluators.nerc.MentionTupleView
documentation:
"
A utility class that represents mention facts as named tuples (without fact ids)

functions.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.helpers.unique_relation_type_id
documentation:
In some datasets (e.g. DocRED) relations can be between different sets of source and target entity types. To meet requirements of
domain we construct unique relation type ids for them (e.g. rel.type_id=f'{rel.type_id}::{source.type_id}_{target.type_id}').

functions.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.helpers.get_original_type_id
documentation:
In some datasets (e.g. DocRED) relations can be between different sets of source and target entity types. To meet requirements of
domain we construct unique relation type ids for them (e.g. rel.type_id=f'{rel.type_id}::{source.type_id}_{target.type_id}').
This function returns original relation type id.

functions.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.helpers.get_domain_type
documentation:
Returns fact's domain type (or build fake one).
NOTE: each type must have a unique id.

functions.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.helpers.build_domain
documentation:
Builds domain based on the facts from gold documents.
In the case if type_id of some fact is string, fake AbstractDomainType is created, otherwise original type_id is used.

functions.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.helpers.replace_with_domain
documentation: Creates a new facts with domain type instead of its identifier.

functions.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.helpers.filter_facts
documentation: Drops facts from document that are not represented in the domain.

functions.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.evaluators.disambiguation_quality.evaluate_dmb
documentation:
---
param at_ks: Compute micro_recall@k for all k from at_ks
param eval_config: optional evaluation config
param gold: Sequence of unambiguous Documents with gold concept fact values
param mode: |-
  "default" | "ambiguous" | "non_ambigous".
  If "default", evaluate all mentions.
  If "ambigous" evaluate only ambigous mentions (with at least 2 predicted concepts)
  If "unambigous" evaluate only unambigous mentions (with at most 1 predicted concept)
param only_in_kb: Evaluate only in KB mentions
param predicted: Sequence of disambiguated Documents with ranked concept fact values
  ranked_concepts
return: |-
  dictionary with keys:
  "num_mentions" -- number of evaluated mentions
  "micro_recall@k" -- value of micro_recall at k for each k from at_ks param
  "mean_reciprocal_rank" -- value of mean reciprocal rank
---

functions.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.action.evaluate
documentation: Core function that evaluates a document processor under the given task.

classes.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.evaluators.relext.RelationTupleView

methods.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.evaluators.relext.RelationTupleView.__post_init__
documentation: Replace domain type id of relation with the original one.

"
A utility class that represents relation facts as dataclasses (without fact ids)

functions.name: talisman_tools.helper.multiprocessing.setup_multiprocessing
documentation:
Conventional forking is not safe in multithreaded environment. Use this function in __main__ to set up thread-safe version
of spawning new processes.

See https://docs.python.org/3/library/multiprocessing.html#contexts-and-start-methods

functions.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.metrics.f1_score
documentation: copy of derek.common.evaluation.metrics

functions.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.metrics._trace
documentation:
Return the partition of `#cluster` induced by `#partition`, that is
```math
\{C∩A|A∈P\} ∪ \{\{x\}|x∈C∖∪P\}
```
Where `$C$` is `#cluster` and `$P$` is `#partition`.
This assume that the elements of `#partition` are indeed pairwise disjoint.
following this are coreference metrics adapted from scorch 0.2.0

functions.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.metrics.remap_clusterings
documentation: Remap clusterings of arbitrary elements to clusterings of integers.

functions.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.metrics.muc_score
documentation:
Compute the MUC `$(R, P, F₁)$` scores for a `#response` clustering given a `#key` clustering,
that is
```math
R &= \frac{∑_{k∈K}(\#k-\#p(k, R))}{∑_{k∈K}(\#k-1)}\\
P &= \frac{∑_{r∈R}(\#r-\#p(r, K))}{∑_{r∈R}(\#r-1)}\\
F &= 2*\frac{PR}{P+R}
```
with `$p(x, E)=\{x∩A|A∈E\}$`.
In the edge case where all clusters in either `#key` or `#response` are singletons, `$P$`, `$R$`
and `$F$` are defined to be `$0$`, following the reference implementation (since singleton
clusters where not considered in Vilain et al. (1995).
Note: This implementation is significantly different from the reference one (despite
implementing the formulae from Pradahan et al. (2014) in that the reference use the ordering of
mentions in documents to consistently assign a non-problematic spanning tree (viz. a chain) to
each cluster, thus avoiding the issues that led Vilain et al. (1995) to define MUC by the
formulae above.

functions.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.metrics.bcub_score
documentation:
Compute the B³ `$(R, P, F₁)$` scores for a `#response` clustering given a `#key` clustering,
that is
```math
R &= \frac{∑_{k∈K}∑_{r∈R}\frac{(\#k∩r)²}{\#k}}{∑_{k∈K}\#k}\\
P &= \frac{∑_{r∈R}∑_{k∈K}\frac{(\#r∩k)²}{\#r}}{∑_{r∈R}\#r}\\
F &= 2*\frac{PR}{P+R}
```

functions.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.metrics.ceaf_score
documentation:
Compute the CEAF `$(R, P, F₁)$` scores for a `#response` clustering given a `#key` clustering
using the `#score` alignment score function, that is
```math
R &= \frac{∑_{k∈K}C(k, A(k))}{∑_{k∈K}C(k, k)}\\
P &= \frac{∑_{r∈R}C(r, A⁻¹(r))}{∑_{r∈R}C(r, r)}\\
F &= 2*\frac{PR}{P+R}
```
Where `$C$` is `#score` and `$A$` is a one-to-one mapping from key clusters to response
clusters that maximizes `$∑_{k∈K}C(k, A(k))$`.

functions.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.metrics.ceafe_score
documentation:
Compute the CEAFₑ `$(R, P, F₁)$` scores for a `#response` clustering given a `#key`
clustering, that is the CEAF score for the `$Φ₄$` score function (aka the Sørensen–Dice
coefficient).
```math
Φ₄: (k, r) ⟼ \frac{2×\#k∩r}{\#k+\#r}
```
Note: this use the original (Luo, 2005) definition as opposed to Pradhan et al. (2014)'s one
which inlines the denominators.

functions.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.metrics.conll2012_score
documentation:
Return the CoNLL-2012 scores for a `#response` clustering given a `#key` clustering, that is,
the average of the MUC, B³ and CEAFₑ scores.

functions.name: talisman_tools.commands.model.commands.evaluate.metrics.get_mention_assignments
documentation: lea section adapted from CoVal at github.com/ns-moosavi/coval
